title: systemd: expect full path /usr/sbin/init
body:
"init" is a quite common name for the command executed in a container
image and Podman ends up using the systemd mode also when not
required.

Be stricter on enabling the systemd mode and not enable it
automatically when the basename is "init" but expect the full path
"/usr/sbin/init".

Signed-off-by: Giuseppe Scrivano <gscrivan@redhat.com>

Should we add a way to explicitly request systemd mode regardless of
command?

> Should we add a way to explicitly request systemd mode regardless of command?

yes, I think we should have a way to enforce it.  Probably we could add `--systemd=always`

> Should we add a way to explicitly request systemd mode regardless of
> command?

do we risk of breaking anything if we change the type from bool to string?

Well any one who built a script that said
--systemd=true or --systemd=false, has to be handled.

Also does --systemd=0 --systemd=1.

Basically we have these as valid options for ParseBool.

ParseBool returns the boolean value represented by the string. It accepts 1, t, T, TRUE, true, True, 0, f, F, FALSE, false, False. Any other value returns an error.
Would this break `--systemd` without an argument?

> Would this break `--systemd` without an argument?

yes, that would break
That would break, but it would be very strange since that is the default.  --systemd=true.
addressed the comments and pushed a new version.
